title: Victoria dies
fact: Victoria was Queen of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland from 20 June 1837 until her death in 1901.

title: Franklin Delano Roosevelt dies
fact: Franklin Delano Roosevelt, also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

title: Abraham Lincoln dies
fact: Abraham Lincoln was the 16th president of the United States, serving from 1861 until his assassination in 1865.

title: George III of Great Britain dies
fact: George III was King of Great Britain and Ireland from 25 October 1760 until his death in 1820.

title: Mahatma Gandhi dies
subtitle: Indian independence activist
fact: Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi was an Indian lawyer, anti-colonial nationalist, and political thinker who employed nonviolent resistance to lead the successful campaign for India's independence from British rule.

title: Napoleon dies
fact: Napoleon Bonaparte, later known by his regnal name Napoleon I, was Emperor of the French from 18 May 1804 until his first abdication in 1814, with a brief restoration during the Hundred Days in 1815.

title: George V dies
subtitle: King of the United Kingdom and the British Dominions, and Emperor of India
fact: George V was King of the United Kingdom and the British Dominions, and Emperor of India, from 6 May 1910 until his death in 1936.

title: Theodore Roosevelt dies
fact: Theodore Roosevelt, also known as Teddy or T.R., was the 26th president of the United States, serving from 1901 to 1909.

title: Edward VII dies
fact: Edward VII was King of the United Kingdom and the British Dominions, and Emperor of India, from 22 January 1901 until his death in 1910.

title: Vladimir Lenin dies
fact: Vladimir Ilyich Ulyanov, better known as Vladimir Lenin, was a Russian revolutionary, politician and political theorist.

title: James A. Garfield dies
fact: James Abram Garfield was the 20th president of the United States, serving from March 1881 until his death in September that year after being shot in July.

title: Nikola Tesla dies
subtitle: Serbian-American engineer and inventor
fact: Nikola Tesla was a Serbian-American engineer, futurist, and inventor.

title: Alexander Hamilton dies
subtitle: American Founding Father and statesman
fact: Alexander Hamilton was an American military officer, statesman, and Founding Father who served as the first U.S. secretary of the treasury from 1789 to 1795 under the presidency of George Washington.

title: William IV dies
fact: William IV was King of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland and King of Hanover from 26 June 1830 until his death in 1837.

title: Karl Marx dies
subtitle: German-born philosopher
fact: Karl Marx was a German philosopher, social and political theorist, economist, journalist, and revolutionary socialist.

title: Vincent van Gogh dies
subtitle: Dutch painter
fact: Vincent Willem van Gogh was a Dutch Post-Impressionist painter who is among the most famous and influential figures in the history of Western art.

title: Thomas Jefferson dies
fact: Thomas Jefferson was an American Founding Father and the third president of the United States from 1801 to 1809.

title: Ulysses S. Grant dies
fact: Ulysses S.

title: Jane Austen dies
subtitle: English novelist
fact: Jane Austen was an English writer known primarily for her six novels, which implicitly interpret, critique, and comment on the English landed gentry at the end of the 18th century.

title: Andrew Jackson dies
fact: Andrew Jackson was the seventh president of the United States from 1829 to 1837.

title: Friedrich Nietzsche dies
subtitle: German philosopher
fact: Friedrich Wilhelm Nietzsche was a German philosopher and writer who started his career as a classical philologist and turned to philosophy early in his academic career.

title: Fyodor Dostoyevsky dies
subtitle: Russian novelist
fact: Fyodor Mikhailovich Dostoevsky was a Russian philosopher, novelist, short story writer, essayist and journalist.

title: Oscar Wilde dies
subtitle: Irish poet, playwright, and aesthete
fact: Oscar Fingal O'Fflahertie Wills Wilde was an Irish author, poet and playwright.

title: Emily Brontë dies
subtitle: English novelist and poet
fact: Emily Jane Brontë was an English writer best known for her 1847 novel Wuthering Heights.

title: Ada Lovelace dies
subtitle: English mathematician
fact: Augusta Ada King, Countess of Lovelace, also known as Ada Lovelace, was an English mathematician and writer chiefly known for work on Charles Babbage's proposed mechanical general-purpose computer, the analytical engine.

title: Empress Elisabeth of Austria dies
fact: Elisabeth, nicknamed Sisi, was Empress of Austria and Queen of Hungary from her marriage to Franz Joseph I on 24 April 1854 until her assassination in 1898.

title: Woodrow Wilson dies
fact: Thomas Woodrow Wilson was the 28th president of the United States, serving from 1913 to 1921.

title: John Adams dies
fact: John Adams was a Founding Father and the second president of the United States from 1797 to 1801.

title: Edgar Allan Poe dies
subtitle: American writer and literary critic
fact: Edgar Allan Poe was an American writer, poet, editor, and literary critic who is best known for his poetry and short stories, particularly his tales involving mystery and the macabre.

title: Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha dies
fact: Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha was the husband of Queen Victoria and consort of the British monarch from their marriage on 10 February 1840 until his death in 1861.

title: Franz Kafka dies
subtitle: Bohemian writer from Prague
fact: Franz Kafka was a German-language Jewish Czech writer and novelist born in Prague, in the Austro-Hungarian Empire.

title: Ludwig van Beethoven dies
subtitle: German composer
fact: Ludwig van Beethoven was a German composer and pianist.

title: Nicholas II of Russia dies
subtitle: The 14th and last Emperor of Russia
fact: Nicholas II was Emperor of Russia, King of Poland and Grand Duke of Finland from 1 November 1894 until his abdication in 1917.

title: Chester A. Arthur dies
fact: Chester Alan Arthur was the 21st president of the United States, serving from 1881 to 1885.

title: Grover Cleveland dies
subtitle: President of the United States
fact: Stephen Grover Cleveland was the 22nd and 24th president of the United States, serving from 1885 to 1889 and from 1893 to 1897.

title: Thomas Edison dies
subtitle: American inventor and businessman
fact: Thomas Alva Edison was an American inventor and businessman.

title: Alexandre Dumas dies
subtitle: French writer and dramatist
fact: Alexandre Dumas, also known as Alexandre Dumas père, was a French novelist and playwright.

title: Mark Twain dies
subtitle: American author and humorist
fact: Samuel Langhorne Clemens, known by the pen name Mark Twain, was an American writer, humorist, and essayist.

title: Charles Darwin dies
subtitle: English naturalist and biologist
fact: Charles Robert Darwin was an English naturalist, geologist, and biologist, widely known for his contributions to evolutionary biology.

title: Leon Trotsky dies
subtitle: Russian Marxist revolutionary
fact: Lev Davidovich Trotsky, better known as Leon Trotsky, was a Russian revolutionary, Soviet politician and political theorist.

title: Immanuel Kant dies
subtitle: German philosopher
fact: Immanuel Kant was a German philosopher.

title: Virginia Woolf dies
subtitle: English modernist writer
fact: Adeline Virginia Woolf was an English writer and one of the most influential 20th-century modernist authors.

title: William McKinley dies
fact: William McKinley was the 25th president of the United States, serving from 1897 until his assassination in 1901.

title: Charles Dickens dies
subtitle: English novelist and social critic
fact: Charles John Huffam Dickens was an English writer and journalist.

title: Warren G. Harding dies
fact: Warren Gamaliel Harding was the 29th president of the United States, serving from 1921 until his death in 1923.

title: Wilhelm II dies
fact: Wilhelm II was the last German Emperor from 1888 until his abdication in 1918.

title: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk dies
subtitle: Founding father of the Republic of Turkey
fact: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk was a Turkish field marshal and statesperson who was the founder of the Republic of Turkey and served as its first president from 1923 until his death in 1938.

title: Bhagat Singh dies
subtitle: Indian revolutionary
fact: Bhagat Singh was an Indian anti-colonial revolutionary who participated in the mistaken murder of a junior British police officer in December 1928 in what was intended to be retaliation for the death of an Indian nationalist.

title: T. E. Lawrence dies
subtitle: British archaeologist, army officer and diplomat
fact: Thomas Edward Lawrence was a British Army officer, archaeologist, diplomat and writer known for his role during the Arab Revolt and Sinai and Palestine campaign against the Ottoman Empire in the First World War.

title: Calvin Coolidge dies
fact: Calvin Coolidge was the 30th president of the United States, serving from 1923 to 1929.

title: Charlotte Brontë dies
subtitle: British novelist and poet
fact: Charlotte Nicholls, commonly known by her maiden name Charlotte Brontë, was an English novelist and poet, and was the elder sister of Emily, Anne and Branwell Brontë.

title: Alexander Graham Bell dies
subtitle: Canadian-American scientist inventor of telephone
fact: Alexander Graham Bell was a Scottish-born Canadian-American inventor, scientist, and engineer who is credited with patenting the first practical telephone.

title: Leopold II of Belgium dies
fact: Leopold II was the second king of the Belgians from 1865 to 1909, and the founder and sole owner of the Congo Free State from 1885 to 1908.

title: Jefferson Davis dies
fact: Jefferson F.

title: Lord Byron dies
subtitle: English Romantic poet and lyricist
fact: George Gordon Byron, 6th Baron Byron, was a British poet.

title: William Howard Taft dies
fact: William Howard Taft was the 27th president of the United States from 1909 to 1913 and the tenth chief justice of the United States from 1921 to 1930.

title: Otto von Bismarck dies
subtitle: German statesman and Chancellor
fact: Otto Eduard Leopold, Prince of Bismarck, Count of Bismarck-Schönhausen, Duke of Lauenburg was a German statesman and diplomat who oversaw the unification of Germany and served as its first chancellor from 1871 to 1890.

title: Henry Ford dies
subtitle: American business magnate
fact: Henry Ford was an American industrialist and business magnate.

title: Arthur Conan Doyle dies
subtitle: British writer and physician
fact: Sir Arthur Ignatius Conan Doyle was a British writer and physician.

title: Erwin Rommel dies
subtitle: German field marshal
fact: Johannes Erwin Eugen Rommel, known as The Desert Fox, was a German Generalfeldmarschall during World War II.

title: Leo Tolstoy dies
subtitle: Russian author
fact: Count Lev Nikolayevich Tolstoy, usually referred to in English as Leo Tolstoy, was a Russian writer.

title: Frédéric Chopin dies
subtitle: Polish composer and pianist
fact: Frédéric François Chopin was a Polish composer and virtuoso pianist of the Romantic period who wrote primarily for solo piano.

title: James Madison dies
fact: James Madison was an American statesman, diplomat, and Founding Father who served as the fourth president of the United States from 1809 to 1817.

title: F. Scott Fitzgerald dies
subtitle: American writer
fact: Francis Scott Key Fitzgerald, widely known as F.

title: John Quincy Adams dies
fact: John Quincy Adams was the sixth president of the United States, serving from 1825 to 1829.

title: Robert E. Lee dies
subtitle: Confederate States general
fact: Robert Edward Lee was a Confederate general whose early actions in the American Civil War led to his appointment as the overall commander of the Confederate States Army near the end of the war.

title: William Henry Harrison dies
fact: William Henry Harrison was the ninth president of the United States, serving from March 4 to April 4, 1841, the shortest presidency in U.S. history.

title: James Buchanan dies
fact: James Buchanan Jr. was the 15th president of the United States, serving from 1857 to 1861.

title: Andrew Johnson dies
fact: Andrew Johnson was the 17th president of the United States, serving from 1865 to 1869.

title: Franz Joseph I of Austria dies
subtitle: Emperor of Austria and King of Hungary
fact: Franz Joseph I or Francis Joseph I was Emperor of Austria, King of Hungary, and the ruler of the other states of the Habsburg monarchy from 2 December 1848 until his death in 1916.

title: Harry Houdini dies
subtitle: Hungarian-born American illusionist, escapologist, and stunt performer
fact: Erik Weisz, known professionally as Harry Houdini, was a Hungarian-American escapologist, illusionist, and stunt performer noted for his escape acts.

title: Henry David Thoreau dies
subtitle: American essayist, poet, and philosopher
fact: Henry David Thoreau was an American naturalist, essayist, poet, and philosopher.

title: Arthur Wellesley, 1st Duke of Wellington dies
fact: Field Marshal Arthur Wellesley, 1st Duke of Wellington was a British Army officer and statesman who was one of the leading military and political figures in Britain during the early 19th century, twice serving as Prime Minister.

title: Gilbert du Motier, Marquis de Lafayette dies
subtitle: French general and politician
fact: Marie-Joseph Paul Yves Roch Gilbert du Motier, Marquis de La Fayette, known in the United States as Lafayette, was a French military officer and politician who volunteered to join the Continental Army, led by General George Washington, in the American Revolutionary War.

title: Aaron Burr dies
fact: Aaron Burr Jr. was an American politician, businessman, and lawyer who served as the third vice president of the United States from 1801 to 1805, during Thomas Jefferson's first presidential term.

title: Percy Bysshe Shelley dies
subtitle: British Romantic poet
fact: Percy Bysshe Shelley was an English writer who is considered one of the major English Romantic poets.

title: Subhas Chandra Bose dies
subtitle: Indian nationalist leader
fact: Subhas Chandra Bose was an Indian anti-colonial nationalist.

title: Claude Monet dies
subtitle: French painter
fact: Oscar-Claude Monet was a French painter and founder of Impressionism who is seen as a key precursor to modernism, especially in his attempts to paint nature as he perceived it.

title: Martin Van Buren dies
fact: Martin Van Buren was the 8th president of the United States, serving from 1837 to 1841.

title: Rabindranath Tagore dies
subtitle: Bengali poet, philosopher and polymath
fact: Rabindranath Thakur, also known by his pseudonym Bhanusimha was a Bengali polymath of the Bengal Renaissance period.

title: Victor Hugo dies
subtitle: French novelist, poet, dramatist and politician
fact: Victor-Marie Hugo, vicomte Hugo was a French Romantic author, poet, essayist, playwright, journalist, human rights activist and politician.

title: James Joyce dies
subtitle: Irish novelist and poet
fact: James Augustine Aloysius Joyce was an Irish novelist, poet, and literary critic.

title: Rudyard Kipling dies
subtitle: English writer and poet
fact: Joseph Rudyard Kipling was an English journalist, novelist, poet and short-story writer.

title: Sun Yat-sen dies
subtitle: Chinese politician, physician, and revolutionary
fact: Sun Yat-sen, a.k.a.

title: Alexander II of Russia dies
subtitle: The 12th Emperor of Russia
fact: Alexander II was Emperor of Russia, King of Poland, and Grand Duke of Finland from 2 March 1855 until his assassination on 13 March 1881.

title: John Tyler dies
fact: John Tyler was the tenth president of the United States, serving from 1841 to 1845, after briefly holding office as the tenth vice president in 1841.

title: Simón Bolívar dies
subtitle: Venezuelan statesman and military officer
fact: Simón José Antonio de la Santísima Trinidad Bolívar Palacios Ponte y Blanco was a Venezuelan military officer and statesman who led what are currently the countries of Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Panama, Peru, and Venezuela to independence from the Spanish Empire.

title: Zachary Taylor dies
fact: Zachary Taylor was an American military officer and politician who was the 12th president of the United States, serving from 1849 until his death in 1850.

title: Lewis Carroll dies
subtitle: British author and scholar
fact: Charles Lutwidge Dodgson, better known by his pen name Lewis Carroll, was an English author, poet, mathematician, photographer and reluctant Anglican deacon.

title: James K. Polk dies
fact: James Knox Polk was the 11th president of the United States, serving from 1845 to 1849.

title: Richard Wagner dies
subtitle: German composer
fact: Wilhelm Richard Wagner was a German composer, theatre director, essayist, and conductor, best known for his operas, although his mature works are often referred to as music dramas.

title: Rutherford B. Hayes dies
fact: Rutherford Birchard Hayes was the 19th president of the United States, serving from 1877 to 1881.

title: H. G. Wells dies
subtitle: English writer
fact: Herbert George Wells was an English writer, prolific in many genres.

title: Alexander III of Russia dies
fact: Alexander III was Emperor of Russia, King of Poland and Grand Duke of Finland from 13 March 1881 until his death in 1894.

title: Arthur Schopenhauer dies
subtitle: German philosopher
fact: Arthur Schopenhauer was a German philosopher.

title: Walt Whitman dies
subtitle: American poet, essayist and journalist
fact: Walter Whitman Jr. was an American poet, essayist, and journalist; he also wrote two novels.

title: Davy Crockett dies
subtitle: American frontiersman and politician
fact: David Crockett was an American politician, militia officer and frontiersman.

title: Hideki Tojo dies
subtitle: Japanese general and politician
fact: Hideki Tojo was a Japanese military officer and politician who served as Prime Minister of Japan from 1941 to 1944 during World War II.

title: James Monroe dies
fact: James Monroe was an American Founding Father who served as the fifth president of the United States from 1817 to 1825.

title: Michael Collins dies
subtitle: Irish revolutionary and politician
fact: Michael Collins was an Irish revolutionary, soldier and politician who was a leading figure in the early-20th century struggle for Irish independence.

title: Benjamin Harrison dies
fact: Benjamin Harrison was the 23rd president of the United States, serving from 1889 to 1893.

title: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe dies
subtitle: German writer, artist, natural scientist and politician
fact: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe was a German polymath who is widely regarded as the most influential writer in the German language.

title: Millard Fillmore dies
fact: Millard Fillmore was the 13th president of the United States, serving from 1850 to 1853.

title: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky dies
subtitle: Russian composer
fact: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky was a Russian composer of the Romantic period.

title: Gavrilo Princip dies
subtitle: Bosnian assassin
fact: Gavrilo Princip was a Bosnian Serb student who assassinated Archduke Franz Ferdinand, heir presumptive to the throne of Austria-Hungary, and his wife Sophie, Duchess of Hohenberg, in Sarajevo on 28 June 1914.

title: Swami Vivekananda dies
subtitle: Indian Hindu monk and philosopher
fact: Swami Vivekananda, born Narendranath Datta, was an Indian Hindu monk, philosopher, author, religious teacher, and the chief disciple of the Indian mystic Ramakrishna.

title: Charles I of Austria dies
subtitle: Last monarch of Austria-Hungary
fact: Charles I and IV was Emperor of Austria, King of Hungary, and the ruler of the other states of the Habsburg monarchy from November 1916 until the monarchy was abolished in November 1918.

title: Søren Kierkegaard dies
subtitle: Danish theologian, philosopher, poet and social critic
fact: Søren Aabye Kierkegaard was a Danish Lutheran theologian, philosopher, poet, social critic, and religious author who is widely considered to be the first existentialist philosopher.

title: George Eliot dies
subtitle: English novelist, essayist, poet and journalist
fact: Mary Ann Evans, known by her pen name George Eliot, was an English novelist, poet, journalist, translator, and one of the leading writers of the Victorian era.

title: William Blake dies
subtitle: English poet and artist
fact: William Blake was an English poet, painter and printmaker.

title: Rosa Luxemburg dies
subtitle: Polish-German Marxist revolutionary
fact: Rosa Luxemburg was a Polish and naturalised-German Marxist theorist and revolutionary.

title: Franklin Pierce dies
fact: Franklin Pierce was the 14th president of the United States, serving from 1853 to 1857.

title: Emperor Meiji dies
fact: Emperor Meiji was Emperor of Japan from 30 January 1867 until his death in 1912.

title: Manfred von Richthofen dies
subtitle: German fighter pilot
fact: Rittmeister Manfred Albrecht Freiherr von Richthofen, known in English as Baron von Richthofen or the Red Baron, was a German fighter pilot with the German Air Force during World War I.

title: Alexander I of Russia dies
subtitle: The tenth Emperor of Russia
fact: Alexander I, nicknamed "the Blessed", was Emperor of Russia from 1801, the first King of Congress Poland from 1815, and the Grand Duke of Finland from 1809 to his death in 1825.

title: Beatrix Potter dies
subtitle: British children's writer and illustrator
fact: Helen Beatrix Heelis, usually known as Beatrix Potter, was an English writer, illustrator, natural scientist, and conservationist.

title: Ernst Röhm dies
subtitle: German Nazi politician, military officer and leader of the Sturmabteilung
fact: Ernst Julius Günther Röhm was a German military officer, politician, and a leading member of the Nazi Party.

title: Hans Christian Andersen dies
subtitle: Danish writer and poet
fact: Hans Christian Andersen was a Danish author.

title: Nicholas I of Russia dies
subtitle: The 11th Emperor of Russia
fact: Nicholas I was Emperor of Russia, King of Poland, and Grand Duke of Finland from 1825 to 1855.

title: John Maynard Keynes dies
subtitle: British economist
fact: John Maynard Keynes, 1st Baron Keynes, was an English economist whose ideas fundamentally changed the theory and practice of macroeconomics and the economic policies of governments.

title: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel dies
subtitle: German philosopher and theologian
fact: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel was a German philosopher and a major figure in the tradition of German idealism.

title: Ernest Shackleton dies
subtitle: Anglo-Irish Antarctic explorer
fact: Sir Ernest Henry Shackleton was an Anglo-Irish Antarctic explorer who led three British expeditions to the Antarctic.

title: Ralph Waldo Emerson dies
subtitle: American philosopher
fact: Ralph Waldo Emerson, who went by his middle name Waldo, was an American essayist, lecturer, philosopher, minister, abolitionist, and poet who led the Transcendentalist movement of the mid-19th century.

title: Benjamin Disraeli dies
subtitle: British statesman
fact: Benjamin Disraeli, 1st Earl of Beaconsfield, was a British statesman, Conservative politician and writer who twice served as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom.

title: Jules Verne dies
subtitle: French writer
fact: Jules Gabriel Verne was a French novelist, poet, and playwright.

title: Isoroku Yamamoto dies
subtitle: Japanese Marshal Admiral
fact: Isoroku Yamamoto was an admiral of the Imperial Japanese Navy (IJN) and the commander of the Combined Fleet during World War II.

title: William Tecumseh Sherman dies
subtitle: United States Army general
fact: William Tecumseh Sherman was an American soldier, businessman, educator, and author.

title: Sacagawea dies
subtitle: Native American woman & companion to Lewis and Clark at age 16
fact: Sacagawea was a Lemhi Shoshone or Hidatsa woman who, in her teens, helped the Lewis and Clark Expedition in achieving their chartered mission objectives by exploring the Louisiana Territory.

title: Robert Louis Stevenson dies
subtitle: Scottish novelist and poet
fact: Robert Louis Stevenson was a Scottish novelist, essayist, poet and travel writer.

title: Anton Chekhov dies
subtitle: Russian dramatist and author
fact: Anton Pavlovich Chekhov was a Russian playwright and short story writer, widely considered one of the greatest writers of all time.

title: Francisco Goya dies
subtitle: Spanish painter and printmaker
fact: Francisco José de Goya y Lucientes was a Spanish romantic painter and printmaker.

title: Franz Liszt dies
subtitle: Hungarian romantic composer and virtuoso pianist
fact: Franz Liszt was a Hungarian composer, virtuoso pianist, conductor and teacher of the Romantic period.

title: Wilhelm Keitel dies
subtitle: German field marshal
fact: Wilhelm Bodewin Johann Gustav Keitel was a German field marshal who held office as chief of the Oberkommando der Wehrmacht (OKW), the high command of Nazi Germany's armed forces, during World War II.

title: G. K. Chesterton dies
subtitle: English author and Christian apologist
fact: Gilbert Keith Chesterton was an English Christian apologist writer.
